curriculum_alignment.subject: mathematics
curriculum_alignment.subtopic: transformations of sine graphs (amplitude, frequency, vertical shifts)
curriculum_alignment.seeds_of_change: Use parameters to transform sine graphs, explaining how a changes amplitude and b changes frequency, and apply transformation language to model a real-world periodic pattern such as tides with an SDG 14 connection. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

verse_ality.driving_question: How can transforming a sine graph help us model real patterns like tides and understand their impact on the world?

knowing_world.focus: Explore how changing parameters transforms a sine wave, focusing on vertical and horizontal stretches/compressions and using amplitude and frequency language to describe what changes.
tools: Embedded interactive sketch (Canvas file) for exploring parameter changes in y = a sin(bx)
activities: Use the sketch to increase/decrease a and describe what changes and what stays the same.
Use the sketch to increase/decrease b and describe what changes and what stays the same.
Explain transformations using correct mathematical language (amplitude, frequency, stretch, compression).

human_story.focus: Sine waves model sound, tides, waves, and brain activity; understanding them supports communication and navigation technologies, including accessibility tools such as sonar and captioning systems.
justice_prompts: How are sine waves relevant to accessible communication, like closed captions or sonar for visually impaired navigation?
activities: Research how ancient mathematicians measured wave motion.
Investigate who developed sine wave properties and why it mattered.

expressive_self.focus: Represent what you notice about changing a and b in your own voice, using metaphor, drawing, and descriptive language to connect amplitude and frequency to lived experience.
activities: Write a metaphor for amplitude and frequency.
Draw sine graphs for different values of a and b.
Describe a “loud” sine wave versus a “tight” sine wave.

designed_world.focus: Apply sine transformations to real modelling contexts such as audio engineering filters or climate science tidal models, choosing parameter values to match observed periodic behaviour.
design_challenges: Create and analyse sine wave models for periodic phenomena like seasonal temperatures, heartbeats, or musical tones.
Explain why your chosen transformations match the phenomenon you model.

regulation_supports: Move your hand in a smooth sine wave motion and let your breath follow the wave.
If stuck, walk and speak a repeating pattern (“up, down, up…”) to reset.
wellbeing_prompts: What helps you reset when you're feeling overwhelmed by symbols or steps?

pathways.name: Final Task - Tidal Cycle Model (SDG 14)
pathways.focus: Create a sine-function model of a simplified tidal cycle, using transformations to match features such as increased amplitude during spring tides, and explain the model using mathematical language.
products: A graph of your tidal sine model
A paragraph or narrated visual explanation describing how a and b (and any vertical shift choices) match the tide behaviour
A brief SDG 14 link explaining how wave patterns affect marine ecosystems
assessment_criteria: Conceptual Understanding: Clear explanation of transformations
Fluency & Accuracy: Correct use of parameters in sine equations
Application: Connection of graph to a real tidal pattern
Independence & Reflection: Thoughtful reflection on choices and impact

required: Read and interpret graphs on coordinate axes
Basic understanding of sine graph shape (visual familiarity)
Multiply and divide with integers and simple decimals
Use correct mathematical language to describe changes to graphs

builds_toward: Trigonometric graphs and transformations (amplitude and period)
Modelling periodic behaviour in science (tides, sound, seasons)
